title: carex × leutzii var. xanthina
intro: Carex × leutzii var. xanthina, also known as golden sedge, is a hybrid plant species within the Carex genus, characterized by its yellow-green or golden foliage. Golden sedge has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Golden sedge can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
